dimensions.note: Workflow-scope report. It consumes the @jentz/aws-default-sg-audit finding and scan_error step data already persisted in swamp and makes no AWS calls of its own, so it handles no credentials or secrets. Nothing is vaulted; the decoded payloads carry no secret fields.

info on run start (workflow name) and on the collection summary (findings/errors/steps/skipped); warn on an undecodable artifact, a schema-failed finding (with the failing field paths), the no-matching-step case (listing observed model types), and a degraded run. All structured placeholders; no secrets logged. tryLog wraps every logger call so a misbehaving logger can never break the report.

The report never throws: decodeJson returns undefined on bad JSON (counted as skipped), safeParse failures are counted+skipped, and an unexpected failure (e.g. a throwing stepExecutions getter) is caught by the outer guard which sets degraded=true and emits a still-valid markdown stub. A test drives exactly that throwing-getter path. The degraded flag is advisory in the JSON rather than thrown, matching the @jentz/aws-s3-bucket-audit precedent so the rich findings payload is never lost to a generic fallback artifact. Re-verified after the ownerFromTags edit: the change is a pure tag-key lookup chain inside a value-returning helper and introduces no new throw site.

tests/ holds the exhaustive matrix: collect (decode, unrelated-model-type ignore, bad-JSON skip, schema-drift skip, unrelated-spec-name ignore), compareFindings ordering, renderMarkdown bucketing + coverage-gap sections (auth_expired/access_denied/other), and report.execute JSON payload (findingCount, byVerdict, errorsByKind, skipped, degraded) including an explicit assertion that NO csv/columns key exists and the markdown carries no CSV recipe. The in-use-SG render test seeds vpcTags { Name, team: 'fullstack', Owner: 'alice' } and asserts the owner/team cells render — that fixture still exercises ownerFromTags through the generic Owner key after the VantaOwner removal, so the assertion keeps its teeth and still passes (19 tests pass). The colocated aws_default_sg_audit_report_test.ts pins name/manifest sync, scope=workflow, and the never-throws-on-empty-context contract. Both success and failure paths covered.

Pure read-and-render with no persisted resources and no external mutation; re-running over the same step data yields the same markdown + JSON (renderMarkdown sorts deterministically via compareFindings). Resilient to missing/corrupt upstream artifacts (skip-counted) and to an entirely empty workflow (empty-but-valid report).

Makes no external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only contract is the upstream @jentz/aws-default-sg-audit finding and scan_error data shapes, which it re-validates with Zod safeParse (FindingSchema/ScanErrorSchema, hand-mirrored from the producer) before rendering — schema drift fails closed into the skipped count rather than a bad render.

Creates no cloud resources, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes via context.dataRepository.getContent and returns markdown + JSON; a single shared TextDecoder is reused. Nothing to clean up or track.

Re-checked after the published-surface scrub: ownerFromTags no longer reads the vendor-specific 'VantaOwner' tag key — its lookup chain is now Owner / owner / Team / team, a generic fallback that surfaces an owner contact from any sensibly-tagged VPC. The neighboring teamFromTags (team / Team) carried no vendor key and is unchanged. grep -niE 'sitoo|vanta|aikido|688375758630|m@s\.com' over the whole extension dir is clean. README examples and the rendered report use placeholder account ids (111111111111/222222222222) and synthetic vpc/sg/profile names (prod-readonly, sg-aaa, redis-vpc). README documents the deliberate no-csv decision rather than embedding real data. No real IPs, internal hostnames, real account ids, or infrastructure identifiers in the entrypoint, README, or fixtures.
